Article.ArticleTitle: The effect of sodium bicarbonate mini-tablets in a carbohydrate hydrogel on prolonged high-intensity cycling performance and metabolism in acute normobaric hypoxia.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: The Sport and Exercise Performance Enhancement and (P)rehabilitation (S- PEP) Group, Edge Hill University, Ormskirk, UK. elishannon48@gmail.com.
Research Institute for Sport and Exercise Sciences, Liverpool John Moores University, Liverpool, UK.
Consultant in Sport, Nutrition, and Exercise Science, Southport, UK, PR8 6JQ.
The Sport and Exercise Performance Enhancement and (P)rehabilitation (S- PEP) Group, Edge Hill University, Ormskirk, UK.
The Sport and Exercise Performance Enhancement and (P)rehabilitation (S- PEP) Group, Edge Hill University, Ormskirk, UK.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Conflict of interest: ESS, LRM, KM and CAB have no competing interests. At the time of study design, SAS was a colleague of ESS, LRM KM and CAB, but is now employed by Maurten AB, Sweden. SAS was not involved in the data collection or analysis processes. Maurten AB Sweden provided the Bicarb System products and maltodextrin placebo mini-tablets free of charge. Ethical approval and consent to participate: Ethical approval was granted through Edge Hill University Ethics Committee (ETH2223-0084) and all procedures were carried out in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki (2013) and its later amendments. Written and verbal consent was obtained for this study. Consent for publication: All participants consented to the publication of findings.
